description: SSH server public keys, files that match the /etc/ssh/*.pub glob, must be owned by root user.
rationale:
If a public host key file is modified by an unauthorized user, the SSH service
may be compromised.
ocil_clause: /etc/ssh/*.pub does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /etc/ssh/*.pub, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/ssh/*.pub
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root

warnings.general:
Remediation is not possible at bootable container build time because SSH host
keys are generated post-deployment.

title: Verify Ownership on SSH Server Public *.pub Key Files
